title: SQL Injection
description: Unsanitised user input used in SQL queries allowing arbitrary data access or modification

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: User-controllable data rendered in HTML without proper encoding enabling script injection

title: Broken Authentication
description: Weak or missing authentication mechanisms allowing unauthorized access to protected resources

title: Insecure Deserialization
description: Untrusted data deserialized without validation enabling remote code execution or injection

title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: Application makes HTTP requests to attacker-controlled URLs enabling internal network scanning

title: Path Traversal
description: User input used to construct file paths without sanitisation allowing access to arbitrary files

title: Sensitive Data Exposure
description: Sensitive information like credentials, tokens, or PII exposed in logs, responses, or storage

title: Broken Access Control
description: Missing or insufficient authorization checks allowing users to access resources beyond their permissions

title: Security Misconfiguration
description: Default credentials, unnecessary services, or overly permissive settings creating attack surface

title: Vulnerable Dependencies
description: Third-party libraries with known CVEs introducing exploitable vulnerabilities into the application

title: Command Injection
description: User input passed to OS command execution without sanitisation enabling arbitrary command execution

title: Cryptographic Weakness
description: Use of weak, broken, or improperly implemented cryptographic algorithms compromising data confidentiality

title: Insufficient Logging and Monitoring
description: Lack of security event logging or alerting preventing detection of attacks and breaches

title: Container Escape
description: Misconfigured container runtime or privileged containers allowing breakout to host system

title: API Security Violation
description: Missing rate limiting, input validation, or authentication on API endpoints enabling abuse
